nodes.name: 📋 MAIN — Workflow Overview

parameters.content:
## 🎧 Customer Support FAQ Audio Library
### Automatically Convert FAQ Answers into Audio & Embed on Support Pages

**What this workflow does:**
This template is triggered by a webhook (from your CMS, Typeform, or any form tool) whenever a new FAQ entry is submitted or a batch audio-generation job is requested. It:
1. Receives FAQ data via **Webhook** (question + answer + category + FAQ ID)
2. Reads the **full FAQ batch** from **Supabase** (your FAQ database table) filtered by category or status
3. **Filters** to only unprocessed FAQs (where `audio_url` is null)
4. Uses **Item Lists node** to deduplicate and sort FAQs by category
5. Loops through each FAQ via **Loop Over Items**
6. Inside the loop: uses **Google Cloud Text-to-Speech API** to generate SSML-enhanced MP3 audio (with pauses, emphasis, and pronunciation hints)
7. Uploads each MP3 via **UploadToURL** — gets a permanent CDN URL per FAQ
8. Updates the **Webflow CMS** collection item for that FAQ — writes the audio URL into the `audio_embed` field so it renders an audio player on your support page automatically
9. Updates the **Supabase** row — stamps `audio_url`, `audio_generated_at`, and sets `status = audio_published`
10. After the loop completes, sends a **Microsoft Teams** card to your support team channel summarising how many FAQs were processed
11. **Responds to the original Webhook** with a JSON summary of all processed FAQ IDs and their audio URLs

**Architecture (completely unique — different trigger, TTS provider, DB, CMS, notification, shape):**
- 🔔 Webhook Trigger — receives FAQ batch job request
- 🗄️ Supabase — read FAQ rows (NEW database node)
- 🔽 Filter Node — exclude already-processed rows
- 📋 Item Lists Node — deduplicate + sort by category
- 🔁 Loop Over Items — process one FAQ at a time
- 🗣️ Google Cloud TTS — SSML-enhanced audio (NOT ElevenLabs)
- ☁️ UploadToURL — host MP3 per FAQ (mandatory)
- 🌐 Webflow CMS — update support page audio embed field
- 🗄️ Supabase Update — write audio URL + status back
- 💼 Microsoft Teams — batch summary card (NOT Slack/Telegram)
- 📨 Respond to Webhook — return JSON result to caller

**Setup Requirements:**
1. Webhook URL — connect your CMS or FAQ form to this endpoint
2. Supabase project URL + service role key. Table: `faqs` with columns: `id`, `question`, `answer`, `category`, `audio_url`, `audio_generated_at`, `status`, `webflow_item_id`
3. Google Cloud TTS API Key (enable Text-to-Speech API in GCP console)
4. UploadToURL endpoint configured
5. Webflow API token + Collection ID for your FAQ support page collection
6. Microsoft Teams Incoming Webhook URL for your support team channel

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Webhook, Supabase Fetch & Filter

parameters.content:
### 🔔 Step 1 — Webhook Intake & Supabase FAQ Fetch
**Webhook Node:** Receives POST requests from your CMS, Typeform, or a manual trigger button. Payload can include `category` (to filter FAQs) or `faq_ids` (array of specific IDs to process). Also accepts `force_regenerate: true` to reprocess already-published FAQs.
**Supabase — Read FAQs:** Queries the `faqs` table using a dynamic filter built from the webhook payload. Uses `select=*` with a `status=neq.audio_published` PostgREST filter by default. Returns all matching FAQ rows as individual items.
**Filter Node:** Drops any row where `audio_url` is already populated AND `force_regenerate` was not sent — ensuring idempotency without extra Supabase logic.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Item Lists, SSML Build & Loop

parameters.content:
### 📋 Step 2 — Dedup, Sort & Loop Setup
**Item Lists Node:** Removes any duplicate FAQ IDs (in case Supabase returned overlapping results from multiple filters) and sorts remaining items alphabetically by `category` — so audio files are generated in logical groupings.
**Code — Build SSML:** For each FAQ, wraps the answer text in Google Cloud TTS SSML markup — adds `<break>` pauses after the question reading, `<emphasis>` on key terms, and `<prosody rate="slow">` on complex phrases. Also prepends 'Question: [question text]' before the answer for full context audio.
**Loop Over Items:** Iterates one FAQ at a time through the TTS + upload + CMS update chain. Prevents API rate limit bursts on Google TTS.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Google TTS, Base64 Decode & Upload

parameters.content:
### 🗣️ Step 3 — Google Cloud TTS & UploadToURL
**Google Cloud TTS:** Calls the `/v1/text:synthesize` endpoint with the SSML input. Uses `WaveNet` voice (`en-US-WaveNet-D` — deep, professional) at 1x speaking rate. Returns base64-encoded MP3 audio content in the response JSON.
**Code — Decode Base64 to Binary:** Google TTS returns audio as base64 string inside JSON — this Code node decodes it into actual binary buffer data so the UploadToURL node can handle it as a file.
**UploadToURL — Host FAQ MP3:** Uploads the decoded binary MP3 and receives a permanent CDN URL. This URL is the source of truth used in both Webflow and Supabase.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Webflow CMS Patch & Supabase Write-Back

parameters.content:
### 🌐 Step 4 — Webflow CMS Update & Supabase Write-Back
**Webflow — Patch CMS Item:** Uses the Webflow REST API to PATCH the FAQ's CMS collection item (identified by `webflow_item_id` from Supabase). Writes the hosted audio URL into the `audio-embed-url` field and sets `audio-published` to `true`. Webflow automatically re-renders the support page with an audio player.
**Supabase — Update FAQ Row:** Updates the `faqs` table row — sets `audio_url`, `audio_generated_at` (ISO timestamp), and `status = audio_published`. This prevents reprocessing and gives ops teams a full audit trail.

nodes.name: 📝 Note — Teams Card & Webhook Response

parameters.content:
### 💼 Step 5 — Teams Summary Card & Webhook Response
**Code — Build Summary:** After the loop finishes, collects all processed FAQ IDs, their questions, categories, and audio URLs into a structured summary object. Also counts successes vs failures.
**Microsoft Teams — Send Adaptive Card:** Posts a rich Adaptive Card to your support team channel with a table of all newly generated FAQs, their categories, and clickable audio URLs. Uses Teams Incoming Webhook (no OAuth needed).
**Respond to Webhook:** Returns a structured JSON response to the original caller with `processed_count`, `faq_ids`, `audio_urls`, and `timestamp` — allowing the calling system (CMS, Typeform, etc.) to confirm success.
